qas.a:
1. treat all weapons as if they're loaded at all times
2. never point a weapon at anything you don't intend to shoot
3. keep your weapon on safety unless ready to fire
4. keep your finger off the trigger until your sights are on target & ready to fire
5. positively identify your target
6. never engage with horseplay while handling weapons

1. not required to maintian qualifications on weapons; but must be certified on the SA/LW procedures
2. must certify annually
3. Document trainings & recertifying on AF Form 797's or AF Form 1098

1. free from distractions
2. only live load and clear M2, MK19, M203's, and .50cal rifles in designated clearing areas

1. 4 inches wide (paint, tape, or floor pattern)
2. front is 6ft from the front of the clearing barrel
3. 6ft wide (3ft on either side of clearing barrel)

1. safely handle weapons
2. issue butt first, muzzle up, weapons cleared out & on safe--if the weapon design/operation permits
3. allow one person to approach issue/turn in window/area
4. visually inspect each weapon is cleared before storing.

Small Arms Hand Reciept

acts as a receipt for issue of SA/LW to individuals who are frequently issued an assigned weapon.

Temorary Issue Reciept

act as a reciept for issue of SA/LW to individuals when AF Form 629 is not used

qas.q: which states that you do not have to have current UoF training to participate in exercises, for which kinds of ammunition?
qas.a: AFI 36-2654; blank or dye marking ammunition

qas.q: While using blank or dye marking ammunition, what does an individual have to be in compliance with?
qas.a: AFI 31-117; Gun Control Act of 1968; and have a current DD Form 2760; safety requirements; working knowledge of weapon system; able to identify between live, dye marking, less than lethal and blank ammunition.
